journal_entry.reason: Target pledges a $1,000,000 donation to a humanitarian program and issues a 1-month promissory note. The donation is recognized as an expense (Donations expense) when the commitment is made. The promissory note creates a liability (Notes payable) for the amount to be paid in one month. No cash is exchanged at this time.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Target has pledged a donation of $1,000,000 to a humanitarian food aid program and issued a 1-month promissory note for the full amount. Under IFRS, donations are recognized as expenses when the entity incurs the obligation. By pledging the donation and issuing a promissory note, Target has created a present obligation to pay $1,000,000. The donation expense is recognized immediately (Dr Donations expense $1,000,000), and the promissory note creates a financial liability (Cr Notes payable $1,000,000). No cash is paid at this time—the payment will occur when the note matures in one month. Donations are not subject to sales tax, so no tax lines are added. The entry balances with total debits and credits of $1,000,000 each.
